content:
First put the rectangle in the plane by  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(5,12), D=(0,12).  
Since E is 1 unit from AD (the line x=0) and 12 from AB (the line y=0), and ΔABE is acute, one finds  
E=(1,−12).  

Any line through E that meets AB at F=(t,0) and meets CD at y=12 will hit CD at  
G=(2t−1,12).  
That line cuts the rectangle into two polygons; for instance the “left” one A–F–G–D has area by shoelace  
½|(0·0 + t·12 + (2t−1)·12 + 0·0) − (0·t + 0·(2t−1) +12·0 +12·0)|  
=½(36t−12)=18t−6.  
Setting 18t−6=30 (half of 60) gives t=2, so  
F=(2,0), G=(3,12),  
and  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Answer: \(\boxed{145}\).
